reference.code: function single_number(nums) { let r=0; for (const x of nums) r ^= x; return r; }

problem.statement:
Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Cocktail Shaker Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Cycle Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

Given the frequencies of `n` symbols, print the **total weighted path length** (sum of frequency × code length) of an optimal Huffman encoding — the total number of bits used to encode the whole message.
Given a binary tree, print `true` if it is a **mirror image** of itself around its center, else `false`.

Given an array `nums` and a target, return the **index** of the first occurrence of `target`, or **-1** if not found.

No sorting is guaranteed.

Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Pancake Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.

Given a string `s` of lowercase letters and a pattern `p` of lowercase letters, `.`, and `*`, print `true` if `p` matches the **entire** `s`, else `false`. Matching rules: `.` matches any single character; `*` matches **zero or more occurrences of the element immediately preceding it** (a letter or `.`) — this is regex-style repetition, not shell-glob wildcard matching.
Given a string `s`, print its **run-length encoding**: each maximal run of a repeated character becomes `<count><char>` (e.g. `aaabbc` → `3a2b1c`). Print an empty line for an empty string.

Given an `n x n` grid of 0 (open) and 1 (blocked) cells, print the length (in number of cells) of the shortest **8-directional** path from the top-left to the bottom-right cell, or **-1** if none exists.

Given an array of integers, sort it in **ascending order** using **Strand Sort** and print the sorted array.

Print space-separated integers on one line.
